title: Get Real Value from Agentic AI in Finance and Accounting
description: Apply the specific traits that separate teams getting real value from agentic AI tools from those just experimenting — with practical steps you can implement this week. Based on Devon Coombs' observations across finance teams.
globalInfo: Source: Devon Coombs — LinkedIn post (2026-04-18 scrape). Key insight: teams getting real value start with their existing workflow (not generic templates), treat AI as preparer not decision-maker, build reconciliation checks into every output, save good workflows as reusable Skills, use the right model for the work, and take security seriously with third-party skills.

steps.title: Start with Your Existing Workflow, Not a Generic Template
steps.content: If your leadership team expects a specific flux report format, teach Claude to replicate that exact format. Reverse-engineer your best deliverable and use it as the template. Generic outputs get thrown away; outputs that match your existing standards get used.

steps.title: Treat the AI as a Capable Preparer, Not a Decision-Maker
steps.content: AI builds fast and formats well. It does not exercise professional judgment. Your role shifts from preparer to reviewer — and that review step is non-negotiable. Never skip it, especially on anything that goes external.

steps.title: Build Reconciliation Checks into Every Output
steps.content: Require the same checks you'd expect from staff: GL totals must tie, debit and credit balances must match, vendor subtotals must reconcile, and formulas should be linked (not hardcoded). If you wouldn't accept a workpaper without these checks from a human, don't accept them from AI.

modules.title: Scale and Secure Your AI Workflows

steps.title: Save Every Good Workflow as a Reusable Skill
steps.content: Every session that produces a good output should end with "build a skill for this." That saved skill file is your automation. It turns a 45-minute manual process into a single command you can run next month.
